replies.author: @markdeane-smith9576
replies.text:
@Elvis_Take_The_Wheel Lol, climate change is natural. There is no solid evidence that humans affect the climate - but that is not the point. Research showed that there were periods that were warmer in the past, so SOME sea level rise might be expected. However a recent study in New Zealand debunked the idea that islands like Tuvalu will suddenly be 'covered in water' - in fact, they found that the island is GROWING LARGER. This is due to coral being washed up on the shoreline. 
Hope that explains it.

replies.author: @Elvis_Take_The_Wheel
replies.text:
Right...since climate change totally isn't a progressively worsening condition, right? I'm sure the ice caps will just spontaneously regenerate despite years of drastic melting and sea levels will just spontaneously drop. You probably won't think it's even real. 🙄😑

Or maybe you could have checked the actual facts yourself since, as you yourself say, facts matter...instead of expecting a fluffy happy AI travel video to cover bummer topics like climate change:

"Tuvalu faces an existential threat from climate change, with sea levels in the island nation rising nearly twice the global average. By 2050, NASA projects that daily tides will submerge half of the capital atoll, Funafuti, and up to 95% of the country could be underwater by 2100."

comments.text:
This is easily one of my favorite YouTube channels. Back in secondary school, I used to spend hours learning about different countries through Encarta Premium, and that curiosity has never left me. Watching these documentaries now genuinely brings me so much joy.

To everyone behind this channel—the researchers, editors, camera crew, and especially the narrator, you all do an incredible job. Thank you for creating such informative and engaging content. God bless you all.

I'd genuinely love the opportunity to volunteer and contribute to this amazing team someday. It would be an honor to be part of what you're building.

comments.author: @sandcastlelyndylynneklynch766
comments.text:
I am so happy your video was Recommended to my feed!! Eventho I am a wk late!!  I actually had recently watched an episode of CBS's 60 minutes about 
this Polynesian island nation, Tuvalu! They met w a former Prime Minister Enele Sopoaga who is active in Climate change Globally and Locally & they met w the ppl living there &  are trying to learn how climate change & how it is making their island sink!!?! And what they can do about it!? Also, many of the elders want to keep the young adults there to keep the nation alive! They understand many want to study abroad and make great careers. But they are really trying to instill in the children, that if you go away to universities abroad, to learn more on how they can keep their home island alive, vibrant and active?! For them to get the great engineering, teaching, medical, sciences, biology degrees and careers, etc then COME back and help their Atoll country & to teach the next generation!! 
Etimoni Timuani was the sole athlete sent to the 2016 RIO Olympics, he was the flag bearer of course and he competed in the 100 meter dash!
He was young, but he wanted the world to know the crisis they face too! 
But Your video SHOWCASED THE BEAUTY OF TUVALU,  THE PPL, THE CULTURE and described it beautifully how it began as an island teaching us knew things in a beautiful way!! I appreciated the way you took us on the island adventure! I am now a new subscriber to your channel and look forward to watching all of your channels videos!! 
Thank you!! Blessings from Dayton, Ohio, USA where airplanes,  flights, runways ,automatic starters for vehicles were all invented and were shown in this video!!! Many other daily objects were invented here in Dayton and the Miami Valley Area too! From Cincinnati to Columbus and in-between many inventions came from here in Ohio! (Sadly the VP of USA grew up 20 mins away from me, ugh) 
But there has been great ppl like
 John Legend!!
  ❤🎉 😊🫠😉
